feedback.markdownBody:
# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1107

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1107 — 2026-05-12T18:25:00Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED

## Context
- Manifest: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1107/.pan/review/agent-pan-1107-review-7248d28d/context.json`
- Branch: `feature/1107`
- Workspace: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1107`

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Status | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- |
| security | done | 0 |
| correctness | done | 1 |
| performance | done | 1 |
| requirements | done | 4 |

## Blocking Findings

### [correctness] Ship completion command is not wired to mark ready-for-merge — `roles/ship.md:45`
The new ship role instructions tell agents to call `pan admin specialists done ship {{issueId}} --status passed --notes "<summary>"`, but the CLI still rejects `ship` in `src/cli/commands/specialists/done.ts:31-37`. If a caller reaches the new server branch directly, `src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:335-370` only logs cost and never sets `readyForMerge`, so `setReviewStatusBase` persists an empty update. This breaks the changed role's main purpose: successful ship completion must expose the dashboard Merge button.

### [performance] Ship completion blocks the dashboard request handler while scanning and parsing session files — `src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:335`
The new `ship` branch calls `findSessionFile(sessionId)` and `parseClaudeSession(sessionFile)` before the `/api/specialists/done` route returns. The reviewer traced those calls to synchronous directory traversal in `src/lib/cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts:65`, `src/lib/cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts:84`, and `src/lib/cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts:104`, plus synchronous full-file JSONL parsing at `src/lib/cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts:201`. That makes blocking filesystem work reachable from dashboard server route code, violating the project rule against sync I/O in dashboard handlers and risking stalls for unrelated HTTP and WebSocket traffic.

### [requirements] Ship completion command is not accepted by the CLI — `roles/ship.md`
The role now documents `pan admin specialists done ship {{issueId}} --status passed --notes "<summary>"` as the completion command, but `src/cli/commands/specialists/done.ts:31-36` validates only `review`, `test`, `merge`, `inspect`, and `uat`. A ship agent following the new role text will fail before it can report completion.

### [requirements] Ship completion does not mark the issue ready for merge — `roles/ship.md`
The issue requires ship completion to mark the PR ready for the human Merge button. The new `ship` branch in `src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:335-370` never assigns `update.readyForMerge = true` for `status === 'passed'`, so even a successful direct endpoint call leaves the dashboard state unchanged.

### [requirements] Per-attempt ship cost capture is not wired into the actual ship path — `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:997`
The cost audit path only runs in `/api/specialists/done` when `sessionId` is present. The actual ship preparation prompt still tells the agent to call `/api/review/${issueId}/status` with `readyForMerge:true` at `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:997-1000`, bypassing the new audit path. The documented `roles/ship.md` command also provides no `sessionId`, so normal ship attempts will not emit the intended per-attempt cost entry.

### [requirements] Normal ship rebase conflicts are not short-circuited — `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:990`
The no-rescan instruction exists only in `buildShipSyncMainPrompt` at `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:1026-1035`. The normal `buildShipPreparationPrompt` path at `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:990-1001` still asks the agent to rebase and resolve conflicts without known conflict files, marker locations, or a no-rescan rule. The conflict-heavy ship scenario from the acceptance criteria can still burn budget through repeated scan loops.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [correctness] Ship cost capture is unreachable from the documented completion command — `src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:338`
Cost capture requires `sessionId`, but `roles/ship.md:45` and `roles/ship.md:73` document only `--status` and `--notes`, and `src/cli/commands/specialists/index.ts:43-47` exposes no `--session-id` option. This overlaps with the blocking requirements gap because the actual ship path cannot currently populate the new audit field.

### [requirements] Bail-out threshold is prompt-only — `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts:985`
The prompt now states $5.00 and 10-conflict-file bail-out thresholds in both ship prompt paths. If the requirement expects a hard system threshold, the implementation remains incomplete because no changed code tracks live per-run cost or enforces the stop condition.

## Clean Sub-roles
- security

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1107/.pan/review/agent-pan-1107-review-7248d28d/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1107 -m "Fixed review issues"`
